research.status_summary: Zapier is actively hiring across engineering, AI, sales, and design roles as of June 2026, with no announced formal layoff rounds in 2026 — though Glassdoor reviews note quiet, unannounced attrition and internal cultural turbulence tied to a heavy AI-first pivot.
research.hiring_policy: Zapier is 100% remote-first since founding, hiring across 30+ countries (36 US states + 30 countries globally), but restricts certain geographies and does not publicly offer visa sponsorship — candidates must already hold local work rights; no EOR-explicit hiring policy is stated, though its distributed model is structurally EOR-compatible for countries where it already operates.
recent_news: March 2026: Zapier launched '1 Million AIs,' a free AI education initiative via live workshops, bootcamps, and courses, riding momentum from its first-ever Super Bowl commercial.
February 2026: Zapier shipped 'AI Guardrails' — a built-in safety layer detecting PII, prompt injection, and toxic content inside automated workflows — alongside unified Agents + Zap templates.
March 2026: Zapier published research from 10,000 AI-powered workflows, identifying lead management as the #1 AI automation use case, reinforcing its enterprise GTM push.
research.growth_signal: Headcount sits at ~1,475 (Tracxn, Apr 2026), up from ~800–805 (2025 estimates), valuation holds at $5B with no new funding needed — projected $400M revenue for 2025 (~29% growth) on a bootstrapped, capital-efficient model.
research.fit_for_lior: Strong fit: Zapier's AI Agents, Copilot (natural language workflow builder), and no-code AI orchestration platform sit squarely at the intersection of Lior's Creative AI, Vibe Coder, and Product Analyst threads — and the company explicitly welcomes AI tool use during its own hiring process.
